name: Empty Mountains - circular by Javalambre
region_intro: Touring cycling around Camarena De La Sierra is characterized by its high-mountain environment within the Sierra de Javalambre range. The region features extensive pine and juniper forests, providing varied terrain for cyclists. Notable natural features include Pico Javalambre and the Vía Verde de Ojos Negros, offering both challenging ascents and gentler paths. This area presents diverse landscapes for touring cyclists seeking routes through mountainous and forested areas. Best touring cycling routes around Camarena De La Sierra The most popular touring cycling route is Empty Mountains - circular by Javalambre, a 20.5 miles (33.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 39 minutes to complete, offering significant elevation gain through mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Empty Mountains - circular through the Puebla de San Miguel Natural Park, a difficult 25.0 miles (40.3 km) path that winds through the natural park's diverse landscapes. Local touring cyclists also love the Red Plain – View of the "Wild West" loop from Puebla de Valverde, a 57.3 miles (92.3 km) trail leading through open plains and offering expansive views, often completed in about 5 hours 48 minutes. Touring cycling around Camarena De La Sierra is defined by its high-mountain environment, extensive pine and juniper forests, and sections of the Vía Verde de Ojos Negros. The network primarily offers challenging routes for experienced cyclists. The routes in Camarena De La Sierra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 190 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Camarena De La Sierra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Camarena De La Sierra?
A: There are 10 touring cycling routes documented on komoot for Camarena De La Sierra. These routes primarily offer challenging experiences through the high-mountain environment of the Sierra de Javalambre.

Q: Are there any family-friendly or easy touring cycling routes in Camarena De La Sierra?
A: The touring cycling routes around Camarena De La Sierra are generally considered difficult, featuring significant elevation gains and mountainous terrain. While the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5400962" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de Ojos Negros&lt;/a&gt;, which has sections in the region, is known for its gentle gradients, the specific touring cycling routes listed on komoot for this area are geared towards experienced cyclists.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while cycling in Camarena De La Sierra?
A: Cyclists in Camarena De La Sierra will experience a genuine high-mountain environment within the Sierra de Javalambre. The routes traverse extensive pine and savin juniper forests, offering shaded paths and tranquil atmospheres. You'll also encounter diverse geological formations, ravines, and potentially panoramic views from peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3925573" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Javalambre&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, several circular routes are available. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e955771784/empty-mountains-circular-by-javalambre" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Empty Mountains - circular by Javalambre&lt;/a&gt; is a 33 km loop, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e956347125/empty-mountains-circular-through-the-puebla-de-san-miguel-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Empty Mountains - circular through the Puebla de San Miguel Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; offers a 40.3 km circular path.

Q: What are some notable natural features or attractions to look out for along the cycling routes?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3925573" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Javalambre&lt;/a&gt;, the highest point in the range, or sections of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5400962" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojos Negros Greenway&lt;/a&gt;. The area is also known as the 'village of the 100 springs,' with natural springs and waterfalls like the Amanaderos Waterfalls, though these may require detours from cycling paths.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Camarena De La Sierra?
A: The touring cycling routes in Camarena De La Sierra are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 20 reviews. Cyclists often praise the challenging nature of the terrain and the stunning high-mountain scenery.

Q: Are there any long-distance touring cycling routes available?
A: Yes, for those seeking longer distances, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31513125" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Red Plain – View of the "Wild West" loop from Puebla de Valverde&lt;/a&gt; spans 92.3 km, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38215809" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Javalambre – Viaducto de Peñaflores loop from Camarena de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt; covers 91.3 km, offering extensive rides through varied landscapes.

Q: Can I combine cycling with other activities, such as visiting cultural sites?
A: Absolutely. While cycling, you can explore cultural and historical points of interest in the village of Camarena de la Sierra, such as the 17th-century baroque Church of San Mateo or the Mudejar-style hermitage of San Roque. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2305519" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Javalambre Astrophysical Observatory&lt;/a&gt; is also a unique nearby attraction.

Q: What is the difficulty level of touring cycling in Camarena De La Sierra?
A: All 10 touring cycling routes listed on komoot for Camarena De La Sierra are rated as 'difficult.' They feature significant elevation gains and are best suited for experienced cyclists looking for a challenging high-mountain adventure.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints or scenic spots along the routes?
A: Many routes offer expansive views, particularly those ascending towards &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3925573" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Javalambre&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31513125" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Red Plain – View of the "Wild West" loop&lt;/a&gt; is specifically noted for its open plains and broad vistas, providing a sense of the region's vastness.

Q: Is there a route that includes the Vía Verde de Ojos Negros?
A: While the Vía Verde de Ojos Negros is a significant feature of the region, the specific touring cycling routes listed on komoot for Camarena De La Sierra focus on the mountainous terrain. However, sections of the Vía Verde, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3925696" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;final stretch of the Escandón pass&lt;/a&gt;, are part of the broader cycling network in the area.
tips:
On the Montañas Vacías route you will ride close to the Refugio Rabadá y Navarro, with guard, next to the Javalambre ski station, where you can also have dinner and breakfast.

It is advisable to call to book if you plan to spend the night there.

Tel:0034 978 768 083 rynrefugio@gmail.com
This refugio at 1,720 metres altitude on the Sierra de Javalambre is basic, but the roof is completely new and the interior has been restored. It has a special charm.
This is one of the most important massifs of the Iberian System, with several of its peaks exceeding 2000 m.
